rulings.Case Title: CHARLES TOBEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant E.I. DuPont de Nemours and Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. DuPont failed to provide evidence of plaintiff¿s factually-devoid responses to contention interrogatories in its separate statement. While DuPont provided this evidence in its reply, the court does not consider additional evidence filed for the first time with the moving party¿s reply papers. (San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, 315-316.) DuPont therefore failed to shift the burden on the issue of exposure. =(220/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ANN MEADOWS BALL et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Alfa Laval Inc.¿s Joinder in Defendant SB Decking, Inc.¿s Motion to Dismiss or Stay Action for Forum Non Conveniens is off calendar. The court granted Defendant Sepco Corp.¿s Motion to Stay on Grounds of Forum Non Conveniens in this action on March 23, 2010. =(220/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN BRIAN TRIMMELL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. PLAINTIFFS STEVEN TRIMMELL, SHARON TRIMMELL Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is Granted. No opposition filed. =(220/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendants American President Lines and Crowley Maritime Corporation¿s motion to strike punitive damages from Plaintiff¿s complaint is denied because Plaintiff¿s state law negligence claim is not preempted by federal maritime law. Even if the negligence claim was governed by federal maritime law, punitive damages would be available. (Atlantic Sounding Co., Inc. v. Townsend (2009) 129 S.Ct. 2561.). =(220/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, April 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendants American President Lines and Crowley Maritime Corporation¿s demurrer to First Amended complaint is overruled with 10 days leave to answer. The complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for negligence under California law and is not uncertain. Plaintiff¿s state law negligence claim is not preempted by federal maritime law because, as pled, the alleged exposure and Defendants¿ alleged activities do not have a ¿maritime connection.¿ (Jerome B. Grubart. Inc. v. Great Lakes Dredge & Dock Co. (1995) 513 U.S. 527; Sisson v. Ruby. (1990) 497 U.S. 358; Foremost Ins. Co. v. Richardson, (1982) 457 U.S. 668; Executive Jet Aviation, Inc. v. Cleveland (1972) 409 U.S. 249. =(220/JJM)
